title: toxicodendron parviflorum
intro: Toxicodendron parviflorum, also known as the poison sumac plant, is a deciduous shrub native to eastern North America, characterized by its distinctive white or greenish flowers and shiny red berries. The plant's sap contains a toxic oil called urushiol, which has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Medicinally, the plant has been used to treat a variety of ailments, including skin conditions, rheumatism, and fever, as well as to stimulate the immune system and reduce pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which are typically used externally to treat skin conditions and wounds.
